qa_pairs.question: What happens if a user tries to prevent the automatic purging of files from the `/scratch` filesystem by manipulating file timestamps?
qa_pairs.answer: Staff have methods to detect users who attempt to circumvent the `/scratch` purging policy by periodically running a recursive `touch` command or similar tactics. Such users will be asked to move the offending data from `/scratch` to another location.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended method for safely copying a directory containing symbolic links from `/scratch` to `/project` to avoid issues with link redirection?
qa_pairs.answer: While `cp` or `rsync` are often sufficient, when a directory contains symbolic links in `/scratch`, they will continue to point back to `/scratch` if copied conventionally. To avoid this, it's recommended to use `tar` to archive the files on `/scratch` and then extract them in `/project`. The command sequence is: `cd /scratch/.../your_data; mkdir /project/.../your_data; tar -cf - ./* | tar -C /project/.../your_data -xf -`.
